previous_statement: R well-defined with ||R-I|| <= C eta. Put U:=S^2-I, so ||U|| <= 4 eta < 1 for eta <= eta_0 < 1/4. Define R:=(S^2)^(-1/2)=(I+U)^(-1/2)=sum_{k>=0} C(-1/2,k) U^k. By GT-binomial-series this converges absolutely (sum_k |C(-1/2,k)| ||U||^k=(1-||U||)^(-1/2)<inf) in the complete order-unit space, as in the function calculus of GT-kitaev-spectral. Hence ||R-I|| <= sum_{k>=1}|C(-1/2,k)| ||U||^k=(1-||U||)^(-1/2)-1 <= C||U|| <= 4C eta for ||U||<=1/2, i.e. ||R-I|| <= C eta.
new_statement: R well-defined with ||R-I||<=C eta. Work in the Banach algebra End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2). Put U:=S^2-I; by node 1.1, ||U||=||S^2-I||<=4 eta, and we FIX eta_0<1/4 so that 4 eta_0<1. Apply GT-funcalc with f(x)=x^(-1/2) (Taylor coefficients a_n=C(-1/2,n), center x0=1, radius rho=1): since ||U||<=4 eta_0<1=rho, R:=(S^2)^(-1/2)=f(S^2)=sum_{n>=0} a_n U^n converges in End(B(H)_sa) and ||R-I||=||f(S^2)-f(I)|| <= sum_{n>=1}|a_n| ||U||^n. Factoring one power of ||U||: sum_{n>=1}|a_n| ||U||^n = ||U|| * sum_{n>=1}|a_n| ||U||^{n-1} <= ||U|| * sum_{n>=1}|a_n|(4 eta_0)^{n-1} = C(eta_0)*||U||, with C(eta_0):=sum_{n>=1}|a_n|(4 eta_0)^{n-1} < infinity since 4 eta_0<rho=1 (GT-funcalc). Hence ||R-I||<=C(eta_0)*||U||<=4 C(eta_0) eta =: C eta.
